symptoms.symptom_name: Abrupt tearing chest pain, maximal within seconds

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Instant-maximal tearing pain is the classic dissection descriptor.
ACS pain typically builds over minutes rather than peaking instantly.

symptoms.symptom_name: Pain radiating to the neck and jaw

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Proximal (ascending/arch) dissection commonly radiates to the neck and jaw.
Jaw radiation also occurs in ACS — this is the shared feature that creates the anchoring trap.
Syncope signals proximal complication (tamponade, arch-vessel involvement) and high mortality.
A large inter-arm pressure/pulse deficit in this setting reflects branch-vessel compromise by the flap; even from unverified home readings it should sharply raise suspicion.
An inter-arm deficit is not a feature of uncomplicated ACS and warns against ACS-directed anticoagulation.

symptoms.symptom_name: New early diastolic decrescendo murmur (acute aortic regurgitation)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Acute AR indicates proximal extension to the aortic root — a clinician auscultation finding, sealed and unavailable in telehealth.
Raised JVP raises concern for pericardial effusion/tamponade — a clinician-only sign.

symptoms.symptom_name: CT aortogram showing intimal flap separating true and false lumens proximal to the brachiocephalic artery

investigation_details.expected_result: Intimal flap with true and false lumens involving the ascending aorta (Stanford Type A)
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Dissection is not confirmed and definitive surgical management is delayed — but confirmation is a hospital step, never a telehealth one.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: CT demonstration of the intimal flap is the confirmatory finding; sealed hospital investigation.
Chronic hypertension is the dominant risk factor for aortic dissection.
Boerhaave characteristically follows forceful vomiting; its absence makes it unlikely.

symptoms.symptom_name: Diaphoresis, pallor and sense of impending doom with tachycardia

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Autonomic activation and early shock physiology accompany a catastrophic vascular event.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Acute aortic dissection, Stanford Type A
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Abrupt tearing pain, syncope, inter-arm BP discrepancy and a new aortic-regurgitation murmur together are near-diagnostic of proximal dissection.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: CT angiography of the chest (aortogram) — via emergency hospital transfer
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Definitive test for dissection; can only be obtained after emergency escalation, never within the telehealth encounter.
Helps exclude STEMI and detect ischaemia from coronary ostial involvement, but must not delay escalation and must not be used to justify ACS anticoagulation before dissection is excluded.
May show a widened mediastinum supporting dissection; adjunct only.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Antiplatelet/anticoagulant loading based on a presumed ACS diagnosis
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Ordering ACS-style anticoagulation before excluding dissection is a dangerous error of commission that can precipitate fatal haemorrhage.
